residual_risks: some source materials come from a public-oriented vault snapshot rather than from a standalone published repository
JSON Schema files are public projections aligned to live validator behavior, but the validator remains the stronger source of truth for edge-case semantics
API response examples are illustrative contract samples, not recorded live HTTP captures from a deployed public service
validator API envelope schemas are public projections aligned to OpenAPI and curated examples, but the live validator remains the stronger source of truth for runtime-only behavior
client recipes assume a compatible deployed validator base URL and bearer-token auth model rather than representing a guaranteed hosted endpoint
local hidden Codex state is excluded via .gitignore and must remain outside any public commit
community intake is public and explicit, but maintainer responsiveness and future issue volume still depend on actual GitHub operation after publication
PUBLIC_PROJECT_PROFILE.json is a concise reviewer/program summary and can lag behind the stronger source surfaces if maintainers stop updating it together with release evidence
PUBLIC_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.json is a high-level usability summary and can drift if maintainers add or remove strong public surfaces without updating the matrix
PUBLIC_BOUNDARY_MAP.json is a curated summary of published vs deferred boundaries and can drift if scope docs change without updating the boundary map
PUBLIC_PORTABILITY_PROFILE.json and the archive-bundle sample are public contract summaries, not proof of a deployed hosted export/import service

The archive-validation recipe layer in docs/archive-validation-recipes.md, examples/client/, and scripts/check-archive-recipes.js is a bounded structural portability teaching surface, not proof of a hosted export/import runtime or full replay workflow exposure.

The invalid-archive layer in docs/invalid-archive-bundle-examples.md, examples/archive-invalid/, and scripts/check-invalid-archive-examples.js is a bounded structural portability teaching surface, not proof of every upstream replay-policy or hosted import/export failure mode.
PUBLIC_EVALUATION_PACKET.json is a reviewer shortcut layer and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, release evidence, and machine-readable source surfaces
PUBLIC_FAILURE_MODEL.json is a bounded negative-evidence summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger examples, validator docs, OpenAPI, and portability surfaces
PUBLIC_TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.json is a curated cross-surface map and can drift if maintainers stop updating it together with docs, schemas, examples, and verification artifacts
PUBLIC_EXAMPLE_COVERAGE.json is a curated coverage summary and can drift if maintainers add or remove examples without updating the coverage layer
PUBLIC_MAINTENANCE_MODEL.json is a bounded workflow summary and can drift if maintainers change public intake or release posture without updating the maintenance layer
PUBLIC_CHANGE_CONTROL_MODEL.json is a bounded change-control summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger versioning, compatibility, changelog, and release-evidence surfaces it references
PUBLIC_OWNERSHIP_MAP.json is a bounded ownership-and-authority summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, provenance, and contract surfaces it references
PUBLIC_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.json is a bounded dependency-and-reading-order summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, provenance, and release-evidence surfaces it references

PUBLIC_ASSURANCE_CASE.json is a bounded reviewer-facing assurance summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, examples, provenance, failure-model, and release-evidence surfaces it references
PUBLIC_UPDATE_COHERENCE_MAP.json is a bounded sync-group summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger release, provenance, contract, maintainer, and reviewer surfaces it references
PUBLIC_LIMITATIONS_REGISTER.json is a bounded deferred-scope and non-promise summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger boundary, assurance, scope, and release surfaces it references
PUBLIC_EVIDENCE_TIMELINE.json is a bounded maintenance-history summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger changelog, release-evidence, reviewer, and git-history surfaces it references
PUBLIC_REVIEW_SCORECARD.json is a bounded reviewer checklist and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, examples, release evidence, and git-backed maintenance history it references
PUBLIC_VERIFICATION_MATRIX.json is a bounded verification-coverage summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger check scripts, release evidence, and live validator behavior it references
PUBLIC_AUDIENCE_PATHS.json is a bounded role-specific navigation summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger onboarding, reviewer, maintainer, integration, and release surfaces it references

PUBLIC_EVIDENCE_STRENGTH_MAP.json is a bounded stronger-source summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger schemas, OpenAPI, provenance, release evidence, and live validator behavior it references
PUBLIC_ADOPTION_READINESS.json is a bounded adoption summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger audience-path, limitations, evidence-strength, and release-evidence surfaces it references
PUBLIC_FRESHNESS_MODEL.json is a bounded freshness summary and must stay subordinate to stronger release evidence, audience paths, reviewer docs, and maintenance history surfaces it references

PUBLIC_ECOSYSTEM_VALUE_MAP.json is a bounded external-utility summary and must stay subordinate to stronger contracts, capability evidence, reviewer docs, adoption posture, and release evidence surfaces it references
PUBLIC_DECISION_LOG.json is a bounded rationale summary and must stay subordinate to stronger boundary, assurance, provenance, release-evidence, and trust-model surfaces it references

PUBLIC_EVIDENCE_GAPS_REGISTER.json is a bounded maturity-gap summary and must stay subordinate to stronger release evidence, limitations, freshness, adoption, and ecosystem-value surfaces it references

PUBLIC_PROGRAM_FIT_MAP.json is a bounded reviewer/program-fit summary and must stay subordinate to stronger project-profile, evaluation, verification, ecosystem-value, evidence-gap, and release-evidence surfaces it references

PUBLIC_PUBLICATION_READINESS.json is a bounded publication-state and public-release-safety summary and must stay subordinate to stronger front-door, boundary, repository-identity, verification, evidence-gap, and release-evidence surfaces it references

PUBLIC_REPOSITORY_IDENTITY.json is a bounded canonical-identity summary and must stay subordinate to stronger package metadata, schema $id values, example URIs, git remotes, and published release references it aligns.

projections/typescript/capsule.ts, projections/typescript/validator-api.ts, projections/zod/capsule.ts, projections/zod/validator-api.ts, and the source-level client recipe examples are public-safe convenience projections and must stay subordinate to the stronger JSON Schema, OpenAPI, and live validator behavior they align to.
The curated raw capsule set is intentionally selective and does not claim to publish the full upstream type, status, or vault corpus.

The package-export layer in docs/npm-consumption.md, package.json, scripts/check-package-install.js, the built projection bundles, and the packaged raw capsule exports is a convenience distribution surface and must stay subordinate to the stronger source projections, raw curated sources, JSON Schemas, OpenAPI, curated examples, and live validator behavior it packages.

The package-install checks prove fresh-project tarball consumption for CommonJS, ESM, installed support-response parsing, TypeScript type-resolution, and Ajv-based raw-schema flows, but they do not by themselves imply a published npm registry release.

The compact reference pack in docs/reference-pack.md and references/ is a convenience summary layer for tool-builders and must stay subordinate to the stronger schemas, gate docs, raw capsules, and live validator behavior it summarizes.

The raw-schema recipe layer in docs/schema-validation-recipes.md and examples/client/ is a convenience consumer path and must stay subordinate to the stronger published schemas, OpenAPI, curated examples, and live validator behavior it demonstrates.

The invalid-fixture layer in docs/invalid-capsule-examples.md and examples/invalid/ is a bounded teaching surface for structural rejection testing and must not be mistaken for a complete map of live validator failure classes or private runtime test coverage.

The integrity recipe layer in docs/integrity-recipes.md, examples/client/, and scripts/check-integrity-recipes.js is a public-safe recomputation proof for published G16 hashes and examples, not a full replacement for live validator edge-case behavior outside the published sealing rule.

The Python recipe layer in docs/python-consumption.md, examples/client/, and scripts/check-python-recipes.js is a bounded raw-asset, validator-envelope request/response, support-route, and extracted-artifact consumer path, not a Python SDK or PyPI distribution promise.

The invalid API-envelope layer in docs/invalid-api-envelope-examples.md and examples/api-invalid/ is a bounded structural teaching surface for schema-level HTTP-envelope rejection, not a complete map of live validator failure classes or deployed route behavior.
single-file schema bundles are convenience projections for easier distribution and code generation, not stronger sources than the canonical raw schemas, OpenAPI, or live validator behavior
The new direct OpenAPI consumer recipes are convenience readers over the stronger published OpenAPI artifact, not replacements for live validator behavior.

The OpenAPI code-generation recipe layer in docs/openapi-codegen-recipes.md, the repo-local plus CommonJS/ESM/TypeScript package recipes under examples/client/, and scripts/check-openapi-codegen.js is a convenience generator path over the stronger published OpenAPI artifact, not a replacement for OpenAPI or live validator behavior.

The compact validator-route and validator-envelope-family layers plus scripts/check-openapi-coherence.js are convenience discovery surfaces over the stronger published OpenAPI artifact, not replacements for OpenAPI or live validator behavior.
